fingerprint: scope:the contract requires the reviewer prompt to emit the new schema 'pactum.contract_reviewer_result.v1alpha1' and forbids editing review_proposals.go, but the contract-review path parses reviewer output via the shared parsereviewerfindingblocks, which hard-gates on block.schema == reviewerfindingsschema ('pactum.reviewer_findings.v1alpha1'). scope-in names a contract-local schema constant and parse-input struct but no contract-local block parser/wrapper, and no acceptance criterion verifies that output carrying the new schema round-trips through the production parse path into a contractreviewfinding. an implementer can satisfy every literal criterion (struct+constant introduced, grep passes, prompt-render assertion passes) while the path still rejects the new schema and silently drops all contract-review findings, or is forced to edit review_proposals.go (scope-out violation). add a scope item for a contract-local block parser accepting the new schema, plus an acceptance criterion that a reviewer block with schema=pactum.contract_reviewer_result.v1alpha1 actually parses into a finding on the contract-review path.
reason: The material fix is already mandated: scope-in item 2 requires the contract-review prompt/parse path to no longer use the shared reviewerFindingsSchema from review_proposals.go, which directly forecloses the silent-drop trap (without the shared schema gate, the path must parse the contract-local schema). Combined with the contract-local schema constant + parse-input struct (scope item 1) and acceptance 0/11/12, a round-trip is already required. crf_001's residual ask is an explicit round-trip TEST — a test-thoroughness preference, not a remaining material spec defect; under the precision rule this slice introduces it is advisory, and the executor + code-review will ensure the round-trip test exists.
